root_text.ud7.1:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud7.1:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā sāriputto āyasmantaṁ lakuṇḍakabhaddiyaṁ anekapariyāyena dhammiyā kathāya sandasseti samādapeti samuttejeti sampahaṁseti.
root_text.ud7.1:2.1: Atha kho āyasmato lakuṇḍakabhaddiyassa āyasmatā sāriputtena anekapariyāyena dhammiyā kathāya sandassiyamānassa samādapiyamānassa samuttejiyamānassa sampahaṁsiyamānassa anupādāya āsavehi cittaṁ vimucci.
root_text.ud7.1:3.1: Addasā kho bhagavā āyasmantaṁ lakuṇḍakabhaddiyaṁ āyasmatā sāriputtena anekapariyāyena dhammiyā kathāya sandassiyamānaṁ samādapiyamānaṁ samuttejiyamānaṁ sampahaṁsiyamānaṁ anupādāya āsavehi cittaṁ vimuttaṁ.
root_text.ud7.1:4.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud7.1:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud7.1:1.3: Now at that time Venerable Sāriputta was educating, encouraging, firing up, and inspiring Venerable Bhaddiya the Dwarf in many ways with a Dhamma talk.
translation_text.ud7.1:2.1: Then after being taught like this Bhaddiya’s mind was freed from defilements by not grasping.
translation_text.ud7.1:3.1: The Buddha saw what had happened.
translation_text.ud7.1:4.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud7.1:5.3: Freed like this, he has crossed the flood
translation_text.ud7.1:5.4: not crossed before, so as to not be reborn.”

variant_text.ud7.1:5.2: Ayaṁhamasmīti → ayamahamasmīti (bj, cck, sya1ed, pts-vp-pli1); ayamhamasmīti (sya2ed)
